name: SINONASAL POLYPOSIS
definition: benign sinonasal mucosal lesions benign antral polyp, which widens the sinus ostium and extends into nasal cavity; 5% of all derivative of choanal polyp (following ischemia of polyp with secondary neovascularity

demographics.Frequency: in 25% of patients with allergic rhinitis;

dominant_finding: remodeling / expansion of sinus with enlargement
discriminating_features: multiple homogeneous soft-tissue masses with smooth convex borders within nasal cavity
remodeling / expansion of sinus with enlargement
thinning of bony trabeculae ± erosive changes at anterior skull base
usually peripheral / occasionally solid heterogeneous enhancement

general: multiple homogeneous soft-tissue masses with smooth convex borders within nasal cavity
remodeling / expansion of sinus with enlargement
thinning of bony trabeculae ± erosive changes at anterior skull base
usually peripheral / occasionally solid heterogeneous enhancement
smooth mass enlarging the sinus ostium
